title: The Social Animal
subtitle: The definitive introductory textbook on social psychology — the scientific study of how people think about, influence, and relate to one another.
author: Elliot Aronson
themes: The power of the situation in shaping behavior
Cognitive dissonance as the engine of attitude change
Conformity and obedience — when and why we yield to social pressure
The science of attraction and close relationships
Prosocial behavior and the bystander effect
awards: One of the 100 most influential psychologists of the 20th century
Over 3 million copies sold across multiple editions
excerpt: Elliot Aronson's The Social Animal is the most widely used social psychology textbook in the world, now in its 12th edition. It covers the entire field — conformity, persuasion, cognitive dissonance, attraction, prejudice, aggression, and prosocial behavior — in clear, engaging prose that reads more like a novel than a textbook. Aronson's central theme: the power of the social situation is far greater than most people imagine.
